questions.question_text: Each variable below is numerical. Which one is **continuous**?

options.text: Number of goals scored in a match
options.why: Goals are counted in whole steps, so this is discrete, not continuous.
Seats are counted in whole steps, so this is discrete, not continuous.
Siblings are counted in whole steps, so this is discrete, not continuous.

questions.solution_text:
Goals, seats and siblings are all **counted** in whole steps, so they are discrete.
Time is **measured** and can take any value in a range.
**Time taken to run $100$ m** is continuous.

questions.question_text: Each variable below is categorical. Which one is **ordinal**?

options.why: Eye colours have no natural order, so this is nominal, not ordinal.
Car brands have no natural order, so this is nominal, not ordinal.
Suburbs have no natural order, so this is nominal, not ordinal.

questions.solution_text:
Eye colour, car brand and suburb have **no natural order**, so they are nominal.
Small, medium and large follow a **natural order**.
**Clothing size** is ordinal.

questions.question_text: A shop records **drink size (small, medium, large)**. A student classifies this categorical variable as nominal. What is the correct subtype?

options.text: Ordinal, because small, medium and large have a natural order.
Nominal, because the sizes are only labels with no order.
options.why: Overlooks that small to large is itself an order; word labels can still be ordered.

options.text: Nominal, because ordering only counts when the categories are numbers.
options.why: Believes ordering needs numeric categories; the size labels order themselves without any numbers.

options.text: Nominal, because any set of word categories is nominal.
options.why: Applies a blanket rule that word categories are nominal; ordinal categories are also words but carry an order.

questions.solution_text:
Small, medium and large follow a **natural order** from smallest to largest.
Because the categories can be ordered, the variable is **ordinal**, not nominal.

questions.question_text: The variable **number of people at a concert** is numerical. What subtype is it?

options.text: Discrete, because the people are counted in whole steps.
Continuous, because the number can be very large.
options.why: Assumes large values mean continuous; a count stays discrete no matter how big it gets.

options.text: Continuous, because it can take many different values.
options.why: Many possible values does not make it continuous; the values are still whole counted steps with gaps between them.

options.text: Continuous, because a concert crowd is measured.
options.why: Calls counting 'measuring'; a crowd is counted person by person, which is discrete.

questions.solution_text:
The people are **counted** in whole steps — you cannot have half a person.
A large count is still counted.
**Discrete.**
